tactical_shape: May not line up in the 4-4-2 used against Haiti
Clarke may use a different system; a back three was used against Côte d’Ivoire and may be a clue to the plan
Expected to be under pressure with Morocco likely having more of the ball and possession
Need to be compact and threat on the ball when they have possession
standout_players: Lewis Ferguson praised by Steve Clarke for a second game in succession
John McGinn felt he was taken out in a penalty incident
Scott McTominay appealed for a penalty
fatigue_injuries: Kieran Tierney departed after a second-half problem; Clarke said it was only cramp
home_read.momentum: Devastated by the defeat but proud of the performance; belief remains that Scotland can compete at this level and now look toward the Brazil game and a possible first-ever knockout qualification.

tactical_shape: Likely to have more of the ball and more possession
Described as a really good, top side with power, pace and skill that can open up a game
standout_players: Issa Diop could have been sent off early, according to Clarke
Morocco as a group were said to have a bit of class
away_read.momentum: Came through a difficult game and are viewed as the real deal; a strong side with confidence from results and performances.
